article.title: Apple Turns 50 With Global Concert Series, Animated Homepage Tribute, and a Paul McCartney Finale at Apple Park
article.summary: Apple marks half a century since its founding on April 1, 1976, with worldwide celebrations spanning concerts, a sketch-art homepage animation, and employee commemorations.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Apple is celebrating its 50th anniversary on April 1, 2026, exactly half a century after Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak founded Apple Computer Inc. in the Jobs family garage in Los Altos, California. The company has marked the milestone with a month-long series of events across four continents, culminating in an animated homepage tribute and a private employee concert at Apple Park headquarters.

"Thinking different has always been at the heart of Apple," CEO Tim Cook wrote in a public letter posted to apple.com. "It's what has driven us to create products that empower people to express themselves, to connect, and to create something wonderful," according to [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-to-celebrate-50-years-of-thinking-different/).

## A Month of Worldwide Celebrations

The anniversary events began on March 13 when 17-time Grammy Award winner Alicia Keys performed on the iconic steps of Apple Grand Central in New York City. Over the following weeks, Apple hosted performances, panels, and creative showcases at flagship stores and partner venues around the world, according to [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-hosts-50th-anniversary-celebrations-around-the-world/).

In London, Mumford & Sons performed alongside DJ Nia Archives at Apple Battersea, showcasing their sixth studio album "Prizefighter," which had reached No. 1 on the UK charts. In Paris, Apple Champs-Elysees hosted a pop-up recording studio curated by Ed Banger Records founder Pedro Winter, followed by live performances from French touch pioneers Breakbot and Cassius. Shanghai saw designer Feng Chen Wang present a fashion show at Apple Jing'an during Shanghai Fashion Week, blending heritage and modernity using iPad, Mac, and Procreate, according to [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-hosts-50th-anniversary-celebrations-around-the-world/).

Additional events spanned Chengdu, where pop icon Chris Lee performed and was photographed taking a selfie with Tim Cook; Tokyo, where virtual artist Mori Calliope discussed her creative process that began with an iPod nano; Mumbai, where visual artist Mira Felicia Malhotra unveiled a mural created on iPad Pro; Vancouver, where figure skater Elladj Balde presented his "Wild Ice" video series shot entirely on iPhone; and Mexico City, where filmmakers behind Apple Original series discussed Mexican storytelling on a global stage, according to [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-hosts-50th-anniversary-celebrations-around-the-world/).

In Washington, D.C., actor Troy Kotsur and Gallaudet University president Roberta Cordano discussed Deaf creativity at Apple Carnegie Library, joined by disability rights advocate Haben Girma, the first deaf-blind graduate of Harvard Law. In Sydney, the Opera House projected digital artworks on its Bennelong sails as part of a yearlong "Illuminating Creativity" collaboration, with an original score by Bailey Pickles composed in Logic Pro, according to [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-hosts-50th-anniversary-celebrations-around-the-world/).

## Homepage Tribute and Employee Commemorations

On April 1, Apple launched a special animated sketch-art video on its homepage featuring iconic products across five decades, from the original Macintosh and iMac to the iPod, Apple Watch, iPhone 17 Pro, and Vision Pro. The accompanying message reads: "50 Years of Thinking Different. At 50 years, it's only natural to look back. But Apple has always looked forward, building tools and delivering experiences that enrich people's lives," according to [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/01/apple-50-birthday-homepage-celebration/).

The celebrations concluded with a private finale concert at Apple Park for employees, reported to be headlined by Paul McCartney. Apple also provided employees with commemorative gifts featuring a scribble-style rainbow Apple logo: a t-shirt, enamel pin, and limited-edition poster, available for pickup through April 30, according to [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/01/apple-50-birthday-homepage-celebration/).

## What We Don't Know

Apple has not announced any product launches or special-edition hardware tied to the anniversary. Whether the company will make additional anniversary-related announcements in the coming weeks, or whether the celebrations are now complete, remains unclear. The company's approach of focusing on cultural events and creative showcases rather than product reveals represents an unusual departure for a corporation that typically orients its public messaging around hardware and software launches.
